description_long: This bungalow is a sprawling, single-story residential building with a complex, multi-winged layout. The structure features a series of interconnected gabled roofs, all finished in a dark, matte material, likely asphalt shingles. The exterior walls are clad in warm-toned wood, giving the building a rustic yet refined appearance. Multiple large windows and glass doors are distributed across the facades, allowing for ample natural light and views. The front entrance is accentuated by a covered porch with timber supports, while paved walkways and a driveway surround the building, set within a neatly manicured lawn. The overall design suggests a blend of Craftsman and lodge architectural influences, emphasizing comfort and connection to the outdoors.
description: A large, single-story wooden bungalow with multiple gabled roofs, expansive windows, and a prominent covered porch, set on a landscaped plot with paved walkways.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance with a covered porch, symmetrical gabled wings, and large windows flanking the entryway.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the back wings and additional entrances, with less emphasis on decorative features and more on utility access.
description_view_2: The side view reveals the depth of the building, multiple gabled sections, and several large glass doors and windows.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the extended wing and secondary entrance, with visible paved paths and a continuation of the gabled roofline.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped front and side windows
